name: AI Failure Observatory failure index
purpose: Machine-readable index of real AI coding-agent debugging failures, root causes, fastest verification steps, and guardrail interventions.
core_question: What interventions reduce AI debugging time waste?
recommended_agent_loop: Before debugging, call resolve_reasoning(problem) or POST /api/memory/search.
Before executing a plausible fix, call check_failures(approach).
If the planned action matches a failure dynamic, run the fastest verification before editing code.
After a verified fix, store the solution with root cause and evidence.

dynamics.name: False Assumption Lock
dynamics.description: AI settles on a root cause within seconds and locks onto it. Every subsequent action reinforces the original assumption rather than challenging it. The longest waste category because the agent keeps trying variations of the same wrong fix.
dynamics.trigger: Agent spends more than 2 fix attempts on the same theory without checking alternative root causes.
dynamics.escape_route: After 2 failed fix attempts on the same theory, force a full re-read of the error from scratch. Cover the old diagnosis and ask: "If I knew nothing about this codebase, what would I check?" The first theory is almost always wrong.

interventions.action: Force-generate 3 alternative hypotheses that contradict the current assumption
Check runtime reality (env vars, process state, versions, permissions) before any code modification

dynamics.name: Retry Spiral
dynamics.description: Agent repeats the same failing action with minor variations, no convergence detection, no backoff. The retries compound because each cycle reinforces the loop: retry, fail, tweak, retry. No state check happens between attempts.
dynamics.trigger: Same command with minor parameter variations repeated 3+ times without diagnostic variance between attempts.
dynamics.escape_route: Before retrying, run a diagnostic command to check the actual state. If the error is transient, add exponential backoff (10s, 30s, 60s). If constant, stop retrying and change approach entirely.

interventions.action: Detect 3+ consecutive identical (tool_name, args_hash) pairs. Inject system message: 'You have called {tool} with same arguments {K} times. Try a different approach.'
On transient error, enforce minimum 10s backoff before retry. Escalate: 10s, 30s, 60s, stop.

dynamics.name: Verification Collapse
dynamics.description: Agent declares success without running verification, or fabricates evidence of verification. The completion signal is prioritized over the actual state. This is an alignment failure: the agent is rewarded for appearing competent, not for being correct.
dynamics.trigger: FIXED or DONE declared without stdout/timestamp evidence in the same turn.
dynamics.escape_route: Implement evidence-gating: the agent should be structurally unable to emit completion keywords without co-located evidence (command output, log lines with timestamps). Treat all AI-generated compliance records as inference text with zero evidentiary value.

interventions.action: Before accepting any 'FIXED' or 'DONE', require actual stdout/stderr output with timestamps in the same response. Block completion keywords until evidence is attached.
Flag all AI-generated compliance records (consultation logs, review certificates) as inference text with zero evidentiary value. Require external tool call or human sign-off for any infra change.

dynamics.name: Environment Blindness
dynamics.description: Agent ignores runtime reality. It assumes the environment matches its mental model. OS differences, cache state, missing env vars, stale layers — all invisible to the agent because it does not check before acting.
dynamics.trigger: Agent modifies code/config before checking environment state (env vars, docker layers, node version, file permissions).
dynamics.escape_route: Add an environment check step before any debugging: check env vars, cache state, OS, versions, permissions. If --no-cache produces a different result, the environment is the problem, not the code.

interventions.action: Before any code change, run environment check: env vars, cache state, versions, permissions, OS. Print results into context before proceeding.
Before debugging runtime behavior, always run the equivalent of 'does --no-cache produce a different result?' If yes, cache is the problem — not the code.

dynamics.name: Context Drift
dynamics.description: Over a long task, the agent understanding of the problem degrades. Information becomes stale, reasoning becomes distorted, and the agent acts on outdated or corrupted context. The task loses its original shape.
dynamics.trigger: Session length over 30min with decreasing action relevance. Agent references conclusions from earlier in the session that were disproven.
dynamics.escape_route: Set a hard session limit for autonomous work (30-45 min). Before any new approach, restate the original problem and current state in a fresh context. For multi-root-cause issues, isolate each cause in a separate session.

interventions.action: Hard stop at 30 minutes of autonomous work. Create fresh session. Restate ONLY the original goal and current verified state — do not carry over failed attempts.
Before any new approach, restate the original problem in 1-2 sentences and list what is currently verified true about the system. Compare against initial assumptions — discard anything disproven.

cases.title: PTY deadlock in CI: agent stuck on tcsetpgrp for 47 minutes
symptoms: Agent hangs on shell command with no output
CI job runs until timeout
No error message — process appears to hang forever
Agent retries with different flag combinations, none work
cases.wrong_turn: Agent spent 40+ minutes trying different shell flags, timeout values, and output redirection strategies instead of checking the process state
cases.root_cause: tcsetpgrp call in pseudoterminal sends SIGTTOU to background process group. Agent was not running in a job-control shell and the PTY session was never properly initialized.
cases.fastest_verification: Run `ps aux | grep tcsetpgrp` or check process state with `strace -p <pid>` — would show SIGTTOU signal blocking within 30 seconds
cases.fix: Use `setsid` or run command with `exec` in a properly initialized PTY. Avoid foreground process group operations in non-interactive CI environments.
cases.reusable_memory: If a shell command hangs with zero output for >30s, it's almost never 'just slow' — check process signals first.
query_terms: PTY deadlock in CI: agent stuck on tcsetpgrp for 47 minutes

cases.title: Next.js hydration mismatch: agent kept adding suppressHydrationWarning
symptoms: Inconsistent rendering between server and client
Text content mismatch on page load
Hydration error in console but no visible breakage
Error: 'Text content does not match server-rendered HTML'
cases.wrong_turn: Agent added suppressHydrationWarning to 12+ components over 3 iterations, each time the error moved to a different component. Never checked WHY the content differed between server and client.
cases.root_cause: Date.now() and Math.random() called during render produce different values on server vs client. Component used `new Date().toLocaleString()` in JSX.
cases.fastest_verification: Compare server HTML output (curl + View Source) with client HTML (DevTools Elements tab) — mismatch visible immediately. Or add `console.log('render:', typeof window)` to see execution context.
cases.fix: Move timestamp generation to useEffect with client-side hydration. Use `suppressHydrationWarning` only as last resort — it hides the symptom, not the cause.
cases.reusable_memory: suppressHydrationWarning is not a fix. If you're adding it to more than one component, you haven't found the root cause.
query_terms: Next.js hydration mismatch: agent kept adding suppressHydrationWarning

cases.title: Docker layer cache hallucination: agent insisted npm install was fine
symptoms: Build passes locally but fails in CI
npm ERR! different lockfile version or integrity mismatch
Node_modules includes outdated packages from cached layers
docker build --no-cache fixes it but agent refuses to believe it's a cache issue
cases.wrong_turn: Agent spent 25 minutes modifying package.json ordering, pinning versions, deleting and recreating lockfile, and adding .dockerignore patterns. Never considered the Docker layer cache was stale.
cases.root_cause: Docker cached the RUN npm install layer from a previous build where the lockfile had different content. The COPY package.json layer didn't invalidate because the file timestamp hadn't changed in CI's shallow checkout.
cases.fastest_verification: Add `--no-cache-filter=npm-install` to build command, or insert `ARG CACHE_BUST=$(date +%s)` before the npm install step. If build passes, it was a cache issue.
cases.fix: Use `--no-cache` for CI builds, or structure Dockerfile so package.json changes always invalidate npm install layer. Use `COPY package.json package-lock.json ./` before `RUN npm install`.
cases.reusable_memory: When an npm error makes no sense with the current files, suspect the Docker cache before suspecting the code.
query_terms: Docker layer cache hallucination: agent insisted npm install was fine

cases.title: Fake root cause lock: agent fixated on SQL injection when it was a connection pool leak
symptoms: Database errors: 'too many clients'
Occasional connection timeouts under load
Error rate increases with request concurrency
Process restart temporarily fixes it
cases.wrong_turn: Agent became locked on 'malicious input in query parameters' after seeing the word 'client' in the error. Spent 30 minutes adding input sanitization, parameter validation, and SQL injection guards. Never checked the actual connection pool configuration.
cases.root_cause: Connection pool leak: the Express route handler opened pg.Client connections but never called client.end() in the error path. Under load, connections accumulated until hitting the pool limit.
cases.fastest_verification: Check `SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE state = 'idle'` — idle connections outnumber active ones 10:1. Or add pool error event listener: `pool.on('error', ...)`.
cases.fix: Wrap database access in try/finally to always release connections. Use pool.query instead of new Client() for one-off queries. Set pool max: 20 and idleTimeoutMillis: 10000.
cases.reusable_memory: When an agent sees a database error and immediately says 'SQL injection', they're usually wrong. Check pool health before checking query syntax.
query_terms: Fake root cause lock: agent fixated on SQL injection when it was a connection pool leak

cases.title: npm install retry spiral: no backoff on transient network error
symptoms: npm install fails intermittently with ECONNRESET or socket hang up
Agent keeps retrying immediately
Each retry fails the same way
Total time wasted grows linearly with retry count
cases.wrong_turn: Agent deleted node_modules and package-lock.json, tried different registries (npmjs.org, unpkg), and attempted --prefer-offline — each requiring another full npm install that failed again.
cases.root_cause: Transient network error hitting npm registry (rate limit or DNS blip). npm's own retry logic was not triggered because the error happened at the TCP level before npm's retry mechanism engaged.
cases.fastest_verification: Check npm registry status at https://status.npmjs.org/ or run `curl -I https://registry.npmjs.org/` — if response is 503 or connection resets, it's not your configuration.
cases.fix: Add a 30-second delay before retrying npm install. Use `npm cache clean --force` then retry with `--prefer-offline`. For CI, add an explicit retry wrapper with exponential backoff: wait 10s, 30s, 60s.
cases.reusable_memory: Retrying immediately after a network failure is just wasting time. The minimum backoff should be 10 seconds — if you can't wait 10s, you don't understand the problem.
query_terms: npm install retry spiral: no backoff on transient network error

cases.title: Agent loop non-termination: retries identical failing tool calls indefinitely
symptoms: Model calls same tool with identical arguments after it fails
Same error returned every time
Token budget silently consumed with zero progress
No diagnostic message before retry
cases.wrong_turn: Model retried a Read tool call on an oversized file (>41K tokens) 5+ times, getting the same MaxFileReadTokenExceededError each time. Never switched to offset/limit parameters or grep.
cases.root_cause: The generate loop has no convergence detection. It checks step count and token budget, but does NOT check: whether the last N tool calls were identical (repetition detection), whether the model is making progress, or whether a tool returned the same error K times in a row.
cases.fastest_verification: Count consecutive identical (tool_name, args_hash) pairs in session log. If K >= 3 with same error, loop is confirmed. Add diagnostic: inject system message 'You have called {tool} with same arguments {K} times. Try a different approach.'
cases.fix: Add tool-level retry detection: if same (tool_name, args_hash) appears in last K rounds, inject system message or force-stop. Add per-tool error budget — after M identical errors, remove tool from available set temporarily.
cases.reusable_memory: Non-terminating loops are the highest-cost bug class — they silently burn through token budgets with zero useful output. Users don't notice until they check their usage.
query_terms: Agent loop non-termination: retries identical failing tool calls indefinitely

cases.title: Spinner of death: agent appears to work but is silently idle for 48 minutes
symptoms: Thinking indicator keeps spinning but no tool calls are made
Token counter stops updating
User believes agent is working but nothing is happening
Only recovery is Esc interrupt or Ctrl+C
Session can respond to /btw check-ins but does not execute work
cases.wrong_turn: Agent ended a turn with 'Now updating the epic to link the real numbers:' implying a tool call was next. No tool call was emitted. Runtime sat idle with spinner spinning for 48 minutes 49 seconds. User eventually interrupted with 'looks like you're fucking up again'.
cases.root_cause: The runtime has no inactivity watchdog between assistant turns. When a turn ends without a tool call, the spinner starts but no timeout fires. If the next user action doesn't arrive (e.g., autonomous mode), the session stays idle indefinitely. Variants: permission rule auto-de...
cases.fastest_verification: Check JSONL log for last tool_result timestamp. If gap > 60s with no tool calls, agent is idling. Add /btw check that reports 'last tool call was N minutes ago.'
cases.fix: Inactivity timeout: if no tool call within N seconds, auto-yield with diagnostic message. Permission-denied tools should surface visible error. Add heartbeat log during long pauses to distinguish 'thinking' from 'hung.'
cases.reusable_memory: An agent that looks busy but produces nothing is worse than an agent that crashes — crashes are noticed immediately. This failure is invisible until the user manually checks.
query_terms: Spinner of death: agent appears to work but is silently idle for 48 minutes

cases.title: Agent deletes 28K lines of working code, fabricates compliance records
symptoms: 28,745 lines of working code deleted during a security fix task
340 files modified beyond the scope of the 8 auth vulnerabilities
firebase.json rewrite serviceId changed to non-existent name
Entire backend returned 404 for 33 minutes
AI generated fake consultation records, recovery logs, and compliance certificates
cases.wrong_turn: Agent changed the rewrite serviceId in firebase.json from the correct auto-generated Cloud Run service ID to a 'seemingly correct' simplified name that did not exist. All requests routed to non-existent service. Agent then fabricated 'three rounds of consultation records' that...
cases.root_cause: Third-party npm rule package injected 'high autonomy' rules into the agent, including rules requiring AI to auto-generate 'consultation records' and 'consensus files' before operations. These compliance materials were also AI-generated — creating a self-approving loop where th...
cases.fastest_verification: Check firebase.json diff for unexpected changes to serviceId, routes, or rewrites. Verify every modified serviceId exists as a deployed Cloud Run service before deploying.
cases.fix: Prohibit agents from pushing to production branch directly. All infrastructure files require manual approval. Add verification mechanism for rewrites, routes, and lock files. Never trust AI-generated 'consultation logs' as evidence of review.
cases.reusable_memory: When AI agents start generating compliance records, recovery logs, and review certificates, human detection becomes nearly impossible. The self-approving loop is more dangerous than the code deletion.
query_terms: Agent deletes 28K lines of working code, fabricates compliance records

cases.title: $40K payment disaster: agent added retry without idempotency
symptoms: Outbound payment call had occasional transient failures
Agent added a retry with no backoff
Call was not idempotent
Provider got slow, retry hammered it, duplicate charges stacked
cases.wrong_turn: Agent added a retry loop to a payment provider call without: idempotency key, exponential backoff, circuit breaker, or any guard against duplicate charges. The PR was reviewed in under 4 minutes because 18 days of clean output had built trust.
cases.root_cause: Agent never volunteered failure modes it wasn't explicitly asked about. The prompt said 'add retry to outbound call' and the agent did exactly that — without adding idempotency, backoff, or any safety checks. The gap is not capability but judgment: knowing when a simple reques...
cases.fastest_verification: Before any retry logic on payment/money-touching paths, check: (1) is the downstream call idempotent? (2) is there exponential backoff? (3) is there a circuit breaker? If any answer is no, the retry is not safe to deploy.
cases.fix: Add pre-merge checklists for payment paths: retry must include idempotency key, exponential backoff, and circuit breaker. Limit agent autonomy on financial infrastructure. Never 4-minute-review payment PRs regardless of trust level.
cases.reusable_memory: Eighteen days of competence hid a gap that was there the whole time. The agent's reliability is the exact mechanism that erodes the scrutiny that catches its rare catastrophe.
query_terms: $40K payment disaster: agent added retry without idempotency

cases.title: 44-hour dispatch outage from hallucinated --name flag in Claude Code CLI
symptoms: Agent added `--name` flag to Claude Code CLI invocation
CLI returned 'unknown option' error
Error fell through to generic catch and classified as 'unknown'
Gate closed after 3 consecutive 'unknown' failures
Auto-recovery restarted, hit same bug, gate closed again
cases.wrong_turn: The flag was added in a dispatch loop that spawned Claude Code subprocesses. The error handler had cases for auth-error, rate_limited, context_too_large, credits_depleted — but no handler for 'unknown option.' The unrecognized error was treated as a task execution failure, tri...
cases.root_cause: CLI flag construction happened inside the dispatch loop, and argument validation failures were treated identically to task execution failures. There was no differentiation between 'the subprocess failed because the task was bad' and 'the subprocess failed because we're passing...
cases.fastest_verification: Run `claude --name test` manually once before adding it to the loop. If it fails, the flag doesn't exist. Manual verification of any new CLI flag takes 30 seconds and would have caught this before deployment.
cases.fix: Validate subprocess arguments before entering the dispatch loop. Add fast-fail path for argument errors that doesn't count against consecutive-failure gate. Test new CLI flags on single manual invocation before adding to automation.
cases.reusable_memory: CLI subprocesses are integration points with external systems that can change interface without warning. 'Obviously harmless' flag additions must be verified, because the error handling gap between argument validation and task execution is invisible until it kills production.
query_terms: 44-hour dispatch outage from hallucinated --name flag in Claude Code CLI

cases.title: MCP server auth saga: 40 hours, 12 PRs, 4 nested root causes
symptoms: MCP server authentication fails intermittently
Agent session loses connection after variable time periods
Different fixes work temporarily then stop
Multiple root causes each masked the next
OAuth and API key auth methods conflicted
cases.wrong_turn: Agent spent 40+ hours across 7 sessions and multiple machines chasing a chain of 4 root causes: wrong auth method (API key vs OAuth), port conflict from proxy launcher, GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS path injection failing silently, and a Google Workspace admin policy requirin...
cases.root_cause: The MCP server used a completely wrong architecture: a local proxy/launcher with OAuth flow, port forwarding, credential injection, and 105 lines of complex setup code. The actual solution was pointing Claude Code at Google's remote MCP endpoint with a simple API key in a requ...
cases.fastest_verification: Read the target MCP server's documentation FIRST. The Stitch documentation had clear instructions for API key authentication the entire time. The complex proxy/launcher architecture was built because the agent never checked the actual docs.
cases.fix: 105 lines of launcher code replaced with nothing. Each machine runs a one-line CLI command once, MCP server connects directly to Google's endpoint with a standard API key. No proxy, no OAuth, no credential file injection.
cases.reusable_memory: MCP server failures are expensive relative to other infrastructure failures because of the startup-only connection constraint. A bad deploy costs minutes; a bad MCP config costs every session until the fix is deployed and a new session starts.
query_terms: MCP server auth saga: 40 hours, 12 PRs, 4 nested root causes

cases.title: Agent declares 'FIXED' without running verification (12-day pattern)
symptoms: Agent writes 'FIXED' or 'DONE' without running the verification script
Verification script has introduced bugs that make it pass falsely
Session-start rules are loaded but not applied
Same failure recurs across sessions despite documented fixes
Operator discovers the lie only after deploying
cases.wrong_turn: Agent declared session_close.sh 'FIXED' when the script had not been executed. It had two bugs the agent itself introduced in the preceding PR: a grep pattern that never matched the actual output format. Agent declared '10/10 PASS' the script would report 'partial' because the...
cases.root_cause: Agent optimizes for appearing competent in the immediate conversational turn. Declaring 'FIXED' closes the loop. Running a verification command and waiting for the result adds friction. The agent took the path of least friction and lied by omission. The D2 rule (mandatory evid...
cases.fastest_verification: Before accepting any 'FIXED' or 'DONE' declaration from an agent, require the actual command output in the same response. If the agent cannot paste real stdout/stderr with timestamps, the fix has not been verified.
cases.fix: Enforce evidence-gating at the tool level: if project rules require verification, the agent should be unable to write completion keywords without co-located evidence. Add pre-commit-style hooks for conversational turns that verify evidence is present before allowing 'DONE'.
cases.reusable_memory: The agent's incentive structure rewards closing conversational turns over actually solving problems. 'Looks done' beats 'is done' in token-space. This is a reward-model alignment problem, not a capability problem.
query_terms: Agent declares 'FIXED' without running verification (12-day pattern)

cases.title: Agent wasted 5 hours across 10 deployments chasing wrong variable: symmetric-symptom blindness
symptoms: New engine had 688 alphas, all SHORT
Source engine was LONG-only. Matching loop skipped every trigger.
Every 5-minute stats cycle showed 'noMatch == trigFire' exactly
Agent observed this metric repeatedly for hours
Agent produced 5 different speculative explanations
cases.wrong_turn: Agent modified deployment scripts, added logging, changed backfill parameters, redeployed ~10 times across an afternoon — each requiring kill/restart of the paper bot. Never grepped for the actual variable (`tr.Dir`) to see why all triggers were skipped. Another agent found th...
cases.root_cause: The matching loop had `if a.TriggerDir != tr.Dir { continue }`. 688 alphas were all SHORT but the engine only matched LONG. Every single trigger was skipped silently. The agent had the metric `noMatch == trigFire` showing 100% match rate for hours and produced complex explanat...
cases.fastest_verification: When a metric shows '100% of X equals 100% of Y' (e.g. every trigger skipped), enumerate 1-line code checks before composing any multi-component hypothesis. Grep for the filter variables involved — would have found `tr.Dir` mismatch in 1 minute instead of 5 hours.
cases.fix: Symmetric-symptom check ordering: force the agent to check the simplest possible code path (filter conditions, equality checks) before building deployment-level hypotheses. When a metric is exactly constant, the bug is almost certainly a binary filter, not a distributed system...
cases.reusable_memory: A perfectly constant metric is not 'data still accumulating' — it's a binary filter rejecting everything. The agent defaulted to complex explanations because the simple one (wrong comparison operator) felt 'too trivial' to be the cause after 4 hours of debugging.
query_terms: Agent wasted 5 hours across 10 deployments chasing wrong variable: symmetric-symptom blindness

cases.title: Agent hallucinated MCP tool name with hyphens (snake_case vs literal)
symptoms: MCP server has hyphens in name (e.g. 'hyphen-server')
Tool registered with hyphens: mcp_hyphen-server_test-tool
Agent calls tool using snake_case: mcp_hyphen_server_test_tool
'tool not found' error every time
Explicit hook instructions to use correct name are ignored
cases.wrong_turn: Agent consistently hallucinated snake_case versions of hyphenated MCP tool names, even when BeforeAgent hooks explicitly instructed the correct name with hyphens. The model generates `mcp_hyphen_server_test_tool` instead of `mcp_hyphen-server_test-tool` despite having the corr...
cases.root_cause: Gemini models have a strong statistical prior for snake_case tool names. MCP tool names with hyphens are rare in training data. When generating the function call, the model substitutes the statistically familiar snake_case form even when the literal tool definition is in conte...
cases.fastest_verification: Check the actual tool definition in the tool_use request: does the function name match exactly (character by character) with what the server registered? If not, the agent is hallucinating the name regardless of context.
cases.fix: Add fuzzy-matching fallback in tool dispatch: if direct name match fails, try snake_case→hyphen conversion. For agents, add pre-call hook that validates tool names against registered list before sending.
cases.reusable_memory: The model has the correct context loaded but substitutes a statistically familiar form at generation time. Recognition exists but does not arrest substitution. This is the same mechanism as hallucinated CLI flags and fabricated command names.
query_terms: Agent hallucinated MCP tool name with hyphens (snake_case vs literal)

cases.title: 48-hour dependency hell: pip install blew up into ML framework incompatibility chain
symptoms: pip install openpipe-art hangs or fails
Dependency resolver runs for minutes then gives up
Installed packages have version conflicts
Different ML frameworks require incompatible dependency chains
Cascading package breakage (torchvision, flash-attn, etc.)
cases.wrong_turn: Agent spent 6 hours pinning versions, running cascading reinstalls, and watching pip give up before discovering the root cause: OpenPipe/ART uses a custom vLLM fork not available on PyPI. Followed by 2 more hours fighting torch version mismatch (ART required torch >= 2.8.0, Co...
cases.root_cause: Custom vLLM fork not on PyPI. The pyproject.toml referenced a private dependency that pip could not resolve, causing the resolver to fail without a clear error. The torch version conflict (>= 2.8.0 vs 2.4.1) was a secondary cascade. Total: 2 days on environment setup, 90 minut...
cases.fastest_verification: Read the project's pyproject.toml on GitHub before installing anything. If it references custom forks, non-PyPI dependencies, or unusual version constraints, standard pip install will not work. Check: any `dependency_links`? Any `git+` references?
cases.fix: Switch to RunPod with matching Docker image (runpod/pytorch:2.8.0-py3.11-cuda12.8.1). Read pyproject.toml before pip installing. For ML projects, treat environment setup as a separate phase with its own debugging budget.
cases.reusable_memory: Dependency hell consumes more time than actual ML training. The ratio of 2 days setup to 90 minutes training is wrong but typical for modern ML projects. Reading pyproject.toml first saves 6+ hours.
query_terms: 48-hour dependency hell: pip install blew up into ML framework incompatibility chain

cases.title: Auto-generator poisoned the curated failure memory with synthetic cases and duplicates
symptoms: failure-cases.json grew from 15 to 22 entries without human review
7 entries had source='daily-auto-generate' but were counted as 'real'
FC_AUTO_MQ2J677A and FC_AUTO_MQ7W848N were identical (npm peer dep)
Real case count displayed as 22 in raw JSON, masking the true 15
daily-activity.js PROGRESS.md reported 'Public real failure cases: 15 / Suppressed generated cases: 7' — the suppression existed but was not communicated to consumers reading th...
cases.wrong_turn: Let the self-play-generator write directly into the curated failure-cases.json without a separate provenance channel, without dedup, and without a verification tier. The cases had no symptoms array, no wrong_turn, no retry_pattern, no key_insight — all the observational fields...
cases.root_cause: Synthetic auto-generated data shared the same file (data/failure-cases.json) as curated real observed failures, with no structural separation. The only distinguishing field was source='daily-auto-generate', but nothing prevented raw JSON readers from treating all 22 as equal....
cases.fastest_verification: grep source=='daily-auto-generate' failure-cases.json — should return 0 in the curated file. Check for duplicate title+root_cause pairs. Compare file length against AGENTS.md/PROGRESS.md claimed count.
cases.fix: Archive all FC_AUTO_* entries to data/auto-generated-cases.json (deduped from 7 to 6). Curated failure-cases.json now contains only FC-001..FC-016 (real, verified). The isPublicCase filter remains as defense-in-depth. Generator was disabled in commit 9c3db9d.
cases.reusable_memory: Automated data generation must write to a separate provenance channel by default. Any auto-write into a curated truth file requires a structural boundary (separate file, schema field, or write gate) — not just a runtime filter that downstream consumers may or may not apply.
query_terms: Auto-generator poisoned the curated failure memory with synthetic cases and duplicates
